chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Welcome to the most exciting part of this course. We are going to learn JavaScript.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: JavaScript is the programming language of the web and it makes websites interactive.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Without JavaScript, websites would be static HTML and CSS pages with no interactivity.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 1995 in just 10 days at Netscape.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Today, JavaScript is one of the most popular programming languages in the world.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: You can use JavaScript for frontend, backend, mobile apps, desktop apps, and even machine learning.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: JavaScript runs in the browser, so you do not need to install anything extra.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Every browser has a JavaScript engine. Chrome has V8, Firefox has SpiderMonkey.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: To write JavaScript, you can use the script tag in your HTML file.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: The script tag can be placed in the head or at the end of the body.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: It is recommended to place it at the end of the body so the HTML loads first.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: You can also write JavaScript in a separate file and link it using the src attribute.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Let me show you your first JavaScript program. We will use console.log to print a message.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Console.log is used to print messages to the browser console for debugging.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Open the browser developer tools with F12 to see the console output.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Variables in JavaScript are containers for storing data values.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: You can declare variables using var, let, or const keywords.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Let is used for variables that can change, const is used for variables that should not change.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Var is the old way of declaring variables and has function scope instead of block scope.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Always prefer let and const over var in modern JavaScript.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: JavaScript has several data types including string, number, boolean, null, undefined, and object.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Strings are text values enclosed in quotes. Numbers can be integers or decimals.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Booleans are true or false values. Null means intentionally empty.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: Undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned a value.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: In the next video, we will dive deeper into JavaScript variables, data types, and operators.

chunks.title: Introduction to JavaScript
chunks.text: JavaScript is going to change how you think about building websites. Get ready for an exciting journey.

text: Welcome to the most exciting part of this course. We are going to learn JavaScript. JavaScript is the programming language of the web and it makes websites interactive. Without JavaScript, websites would be static HTML and CSS pages with no interactivity. JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 1995 in just 10 days at Netscape. Today, JavaScript is one of the most popular programming languages in the world. You can use JavaScript for frontend, backend, mobile apps, desktop apps, and even machine learning. JavaScript runs in the browser, so you do not need to install anything extra. Every browser has a JavaScript engine. Chrome has V8, Firefox has SpiderMonkey. To write JavaScript, you can use the script tag in your HTML file. The script tag can be placed in the head or at the end of the body. It is recommended to place it at the end of the body so the HTML loads first. You can also write JavaScript in a separate file and link it using the src attribute. Let me show you your first JavaScript program. We will use console.log to print a message. Console.log is used to print messages to the browser console for debugging. Open the browser developer tools with F12 to see the console output. Variables in JavaScript are containers for storing data values. You can declare variables using var, let, or const keywords. Let is used for variables that can change, const is used for variables that should not change. Var is the old way of declaring variables and has function scope instead of block scope. Always prefer let and const over var in modern JavaScript. JavaScript has several data types including string, number, boolean, null, undefined, and object. Strings are text values enclosed in quotes. Numbers can be integers or decimals. Booleans are true or false values. Null means intentionally empty. Undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned a value. In the next video, we will dive deeper into JavaScript variables, data types, and operators. JavaScript is going to change how you think about building websites. Get ready for an exciting journey.
